finding: Token stored in plaintext via electron-store — no encryption or safeStorage. GitHub tokens granting write access (PR creation) stored as plain JSON at app.getPath('userData') (~/Library/Application Support/ai.opencode.desktop.dev). Plan acknowledges risk (fix-B risks) but doesn't commit to safeStorage. Electron provides safeStorage.encryptString() / decryptString() since v6 — must use it or document explicit v1 trade-off with filed follow-up.
repair_path: Add `import { safeStorage } from 'electron'` to ipc.ts or a new github-auth.ts. Wrap token write with safeStorage.encryptString(), token read with safeStorage.decryptString(). Add `github-auth` (encrypted) to RESERVED_STORE_NAMES.

finding: OAuth flow is underspecified — plan mentions Device Flow vs PKCE in risks but never resolves. PKCE requires `opencode://github-oauth` deep-link handler (new hostname in packages/app/src/pages/layout/deep-links.ts). Device Flow requires no deep link but needs GitHub Device Flow UX (code display + polling). Plan must pick one and spec the deep-link routing before implementation.
repair_path: Choose PKCE (no client_secret, better UX). Add `parseGitHubOAuthCallback` to packages/app/src/pages/layout/deep-links.ts. Add `github-oauth` hostname handler in the renderer deep-link event listener. Document choice in plan.

finding: IPC proxy URL validation is mentioned as a risk but has no concrete allowlist spec. The renderer must NOT be able to call arbitrary URLs through the main process proxy — only `api.github.com` (or `*.github.com` for `api.github.com` and `uploads.github.com`). Plan needs to specify the validation function.
repair_path: Add URL validation in the github-api-proxy IPC handler: parse URL, extract hostname, match against allowlist ['api.github.com', 'uploads.github.com']. Reject with typed error if no match. Document this in the fix-E spec.

finding: produce_fragment for DesktopMenuAction union type is fragile. DesktopMenuAction is a string literal union defined in packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts — adding `| "github.createPullRequest"` requires modifying a type definition that is consumed by 9 files across 2 packages. The plan also says to use produce_fragment for the DESKTOP_MENU constant. These are two different edit targets in the same file but at different structural levels (type vs value). Fragments for the same file may conflict.
repair_path: Consolidate both edits (type union + menu constant) into a single fragment or direct edit batch targeting packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts. Do not split into two fragments for the same file.

finding: i18n scope is underestimated. Plan only modifies packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts but 15 other locale files exist. If the i18n framework doesn't gracefully fall back to 'en' for missing keys, adding 'github.*' keys to en.ts alone will cause crashes in non-English locales. Should verify i18n fallback behavior or add keys to all 16 locale files.
repair_path: Check i18n framework for fallback behavior. If it falls back to en, proceed with en.ts only and file follow-up. If not, add stub keys to all 16 locale files or use produce_fragment for each.

finding: Octokit service location creates architectural ambiguity. The GitHub service in packages/opencode/src/github/ uses @octokit/rest directly (Node.js context) but the desktop app needs to call GitHub through the IPC proxy (fix-E). This means the GitHubService must work in TWO modes: direct (CLI) and proxied (desktop renderer). The plan doesn't specify this dual-mode design or provide a seam for proxy injection.
repair_path: Define GitHubService as an Effect interface/Tag with a direct Octokit implementation and a separate IPC-proxy implementation. The desktop renderer uses the IPC-proxy implementation; the CLI uses the direct Octokit one. Wire them via Layer. Document in fix-C spec.

finding: F1 — Token stored in plaintext via electron-store. GitHub tokens with repo write access stored as plain JSON in app.getPath('userData'). Need safeStorage.encryptString/decryptString.
repair_path: Use safeStorage.encryptString() on write, safeStorage.decryptString() on read. Add github-auth store name to RESERVED_STORE_NAMES. Or if deferred, file explicit follow-up issue.

finding: F2 — OAuth flow underspecified. PKCE not chosen, deep-link handler (deep-links.ts) not in plan. parseGitHubOAuthCallback missing. Existing deep-links.ts only handles open-project and new-session hostnames.
repair_path: Choose PKCE flow. Add parseGitHubOAuthCallback to deep-links.ts. Add deep-links.ts to plan's modified_files. Document exact callback URL format.

finding: F3 — IPC proxy URL validation unspecified. github-api-proxy handler with stored token could proxy to arbitrary URLs (open relay). No allowlist specified.
repair_path: Specify URL validation: parse URL, extract hostname, match allowlist ['api.github.com', 'uploads.github.com']. Use URL.canParse and new URL(url).hostname, never raw string matching. Reject with typed GitHubProxyError.

finding: F1: OAuth deep-link callback completely dead. parseGitHubOAuthCallback defined in deep-links.ts but never imported/called. No preload bridge method for github-oauth-callback IPC handler. Deep link handler ignores GitHub URLs.
repair_path: Wire parseGitHubOAuthCallback into layout.tsx deep link handler. Add github-oauth-callback to preload bridge.

finding: F2: hasToken() checks method existence, not token value. !!api.githubGetToken is always truthy since method exists. Create PR button always enabled even with no token.
repair_path: Change hasToken to await api.githubGetToken() and check if returned value is non-null.

finding: [ipc.ts God module] GitHub domain logic (PKCE OAuth at ipc.ts:26-55, safeStorage token management at ipc.ts:42-56, API proxy at ipc.ts:463-491) is inlined directly in packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts. This file is now 503 lines handling init, MCP, agents, plugins, OS operations, AND full GitHub OAuth + token management + API proxy. Fix-B and Fix-E both land here. Precedent exists for extraction: desktop-menu-actions.ts is a separate module. The plan should extract a packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts registered by registerIpcHandlers. This is a structural debt concern — future lanes will keep adding to the monolith.
repair_path: Extract GitHub IPC handlers (github-oauth-start, github-oauth-callback, github-get-token, github-set-token, github-clear-token, github-api-proxy) + safeStorage helpers + PKCE helpers into packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts with a registerGithubIpcHandlers() export called from registerIpcHandlers.

finding: [Preload schema validation] githubSetToken (packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts:124, packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts:447-450) accepts any non-empty string. There's no validation that the token matches GitHub PAT format (ghp_*, github_pat_*, ghu_*, or the OAuth token format). While the primary flow is OAuth (safe), the githubSetToken bridge is exposed to the renderer and can be called by any renderer code. The main process check (`typeof token !== "string" || !token`) is minimal. A preload-side validation would catch malformed tokens early and provide better error messages. Also, no confirmation dialog before storing a manually-set token.
repair_path: Add GitHub PAT format validation in the preload bridge or main process handler: check token matches /^(ghp|github_pat|ghu|gho)_[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$/ or document that it's the OAuth access_token format. Add a confirmation dialog in the renderer before calling githubSetToken for manual entry.

finding: [API proxy error serialization] packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts:465, 471 — github-api-proxy handler uses throw new Error(JSON.stringify({type: "GitHubProxyError", hostname, allowedHostnames})) for URL validation failures. When this crosses the Electron IPC boundary, the Error is serialized as {message: string}. The renderer receives a plain Error with the JSON string inside .message, requiring JSON.parse(error.message) to access the typed fields. This is fragile and undocumented. The handler should return a structured object { success: false, error: { type: "GitHubProxyError", hostname, allowedHostnames } } instead of throwing.
repair_path: Change github-api-proxy handler to return { status: number, body: string } on success and a structured error object { error: { type, hostname, allowedHostnames, message } } on validation failure, rather than throwing JSON-stringified errors through IPC.

finding: [i18n locale coverage] 13 github.* i18n keys added to en.ts (lines 590-602) but missing from the other 15 locale files. The i18n system (@solid-primitives/i18n) loads each locale's dictionary independently — there is no key fallback to 'en'. If a user has locale set to de/fr/ja/ko/etc, they will see undefined in the UI wherever language.t("github.createPullRequest") or similar are called (review-tab.tsx:155,163,176). The plan flags this as a risk but doesn't specify that all 16 locale files must be updated or a fallback mechanism must be added to the LanguageProvider.
repair_path: Either (a) add github.* key stubs with English fallback text to all 15 non-English locale files, or (b) implement a dict merge in LanguageProvider that falls back to en keys for missing locale entries.

finding: [parseGitHubRemote added to RepositoryCache interface] fix-A adds parseGitHubRemote as a method on RepositoryCache.Service interface (packages/opencode/src/reference/repository-cache.ts). This is a domain-specific GitHub function being added to a general-purpose repository cache interface. All RepositoryCache consumers now see this GitHub-specific method. The function already exists as a standalone export in repository.ts and could be imported directly. Adding it to RepositoryCache increases the interface surface for all consumers including ones that don't care about GitHub. This should be a utility function consumers import directly, not a service method.
repair_path: Remove parseGitHubRemote from RepositoryCache.Interface and keep it as a standalone export from repository.ts. Consumers that need GitHub remote parsing import it directly.
